agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: CD-1 rezoning for three sites on East Hastings Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on March 10, 2026 on a proposed rezoning for three non-contiguous sites on East Hastings Street and Glen Drive. The rezoning would allow three towers: a 37-storey rental building (382 units) at 1030-1070 East Hastings, a 39-storey rental building (385 units) at 1115-1129 East Hastings & 360 Glen Drive, and a 19-storey social housing building (157 units) at 1168-1182 East Hastings. All would include ground-floor commercial space. The applicant is Westbank. A staff memo notes parking concerns and states that parking, loading, and bicycle spaces will be finalized at the development permit stage.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on March 10, 2026 — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and development opportunities.

key_dates.label: Council decision on CD-1 rezoning — East Hastings sites

agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.	CD-1 Rezoning: (i) 1030-1070 East Hastings Street, (ii) 1115-1129 East Hastings Street & 360 Glen Drive and (iii) 1168-1182 East Hastings Street
At the Public Hearing on February 24, 2026, Council heard the staff presentation, applicant comments, asked questions to staff and the applicant, heard from public speakers, and closed the speakers list and receipt of public comments for the above-noted Item. Subsequently, Council referred closing comments, further questions to staff, debate and decision to the Council meeting on March 10, 2026, as Unfinished Business.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: West End Business Improvement Area designation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a bylaw to create a new Business Improvement Area (BIA) in Vancouver's West End. A BIA is a defined area where local businesses pay an extra levy (a special tax) to fund improvements like street cleaning, marketing, and events. If approved, this would mean businesses in the West End would be required to pay this levy. The decision is not final yet — council is set to vote on the proposed bylaw at an upcoming meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver property tax relief for development land
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote March 10, 2026 on extending two property tax relief programs for 2026. The Pilot Development Potential Relief Program (DPRP) would apply to eligible Light Industry (Class 5) and Business/Other (Class 6) properties, taxing a portion of land value at 50% of the blended Class 5/6 rate, up to a maximum of $6.5 million per property. The Targeted 5-Year Land Assessment Averaging program would apply to Residential (Class 1), Light Industry (Class 5), and Business/Other (Class 6) properties with a 10% threshold above class average change, also capped at $6.5 million, with exclusions for unimproved, subdivided, or recently rezoned properties. Properties receiving DPRP relief are excluded from averaging. Tax relief is redistributed within the same classes, estimated to increase Class 1 rates by ~0.1% and blended Class 5/6 rates by ~0.6%.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit comments to Council before the March 10, 2026 meeting if you own eligible development land in Vancouver.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver Charter change to renew BIA funding model
agenda_briefs.summary: Council approved proposed amendments to the Vancouver Charter to remove the requirement that a BIA have a finite operating term, remove the 20-year maximum term, remove the limit on total special levy a BIA may collect during a term, and integrate a new dissolution procedure. If enacted by the Province, BIAs would no longer need periodic renewal votes; instead, a dissolution process would be used to end a BIA. The changes apply to all 22 Vancouver BIAs, most of which currently operate on five-year renewal cycles.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Watch for provincial enactment of the Charter amendments.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Auditor General recommendations on land sales and whistleblower rules
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council voted to adopt all 31 recommendations from the Auditor General's reports on land sales and exchanges, and on whistleblower protections. The land sales report includes 10 recommendations to update, clarify, and modernize how the city sells or exchanges land, improve information provided to Council on land deals, and create annual reporting on sales value and community benefits. The whistleblower report includes 21 recommendations to strengthen protections for employees who report wrongdoing. Staff must now report back with a proposed response to the audit findings.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 2026 property tax relief for development and land assessment averaging
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council unanimously approved two property tax relief programs for 2026. First, the pilot Development Potential Relief Program (DPRP) was extended for eligible Light Industry (Class 5) and Business (Class 6) properties. This program taxes eligible land value at 50% less than the normal rate, up to a maximum of $6.5 million per property. Second, Council approved continuing the 5-year Land Assessment Averaging program for Residential (Class 1), Light Industry (Class 5), and Business (Class 6) properties. This program smooths out large year-over-year increases in property assessments. Properties that get relief under the DPRP cannot also get relief under the averaging program. Both programs will be enacted through new bylaws.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your property qualifies for either program and consult your property tax advisor before the 2026 tax year is finalized.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Vancouver Charter changes for BIA renewal
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council voted to approve amendments to the Vancouver Charter that will make it easier for Business Improvement Areas (BIAs) to renew. BIAs are local business associations that collect a special levy from businesses in a district to fund improvements like street cleaning, marketing, and events. The approved changes remove the requirement that a BIA must have a set end date, remove the 20-year maximum term, and remove the limit on how much money a BIA can collect during its term. Council also added a new procedure for dissolving a BIA. These amendments will now be sent to the provincial government for final approval.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Watch for provincial approval of these Charter changes, which will affect future BIA renewals in Vancouver.
minutes_briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body:
3. Proposed Vancouver Charter Amendment - Business Improvement Area (BIA) Renewal
February 10, 2026

THAT Mayor and Council approve the proposed amendments to the Vancouver Charter as outlined below, and direct staff to formally submit them to the Government of B.C. for consideration:

• Remove the requirement that a Business Improvement Area (BIA) be designated with a finite operating term;
• Remove the restriction that a BIA may be designated for no more than 20 years;
• Remove the limitation that specifies the total special levy a BIA may collect during an operating term; and
• Integrate the dissolution procedure recommended in the Report dated February 10, 2026, entitled "Proposed Vancouver Charter Amendment - Business Improvement Area (BIA) Renewal".

ADOPTED ON CONSENT (Vote No. 11454)

A BIA is a local group of businesses that work together to improve their neighbourhood, like cleaning streets, putting up holiday lights, or running marketing campaigns. Council also approved $20,359,547 in grants to these BIAs to fund their work. The Director of Legal Services has been instructed to bring forward the necessary bylaws to collect the money from property owners in each BIA to pay for these grants.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you own a business or property in a Vancouver BIA, check your BIA's approved budget to understand any changes to your annual levy.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for three East Hastings rental housing sites
minutes_briefs.summary: Vancouver Council approved rezoning for three sites on East Hastings Street to allow new rental housing. The approvals cover a 37-storey building with 382 rental units at 1030-1070 East Hastings, a 39-storey building with 385 rental units at 1115-1129 East Hastings and 360 Glen Drive, and a 19-storey social housing building with 157 units at 1168-1182 East Hastings. All buildings will have commercial space on the ground floor. However, Council then voted to send the application back to staff for further study on parking, childcare, affordability, and building design before final bylaw enactment.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Monitor City of Vancouver for updates on the staff study and future Council vote on final bylaw enactment.

Unfinished Business

1. CD-1 Rezoning: (i) 1030-1070 East Hastings Street, (ii) 1115-1129 East Hastings Street & 360 Glen Drive and (iii) 1168-1182 East Hastings Street

MOVED by Councillor Maloney
SECONDED by Councillor Orr

A. THAT the application by Westbank on behalf of: (i) 1046079 B.C. Ltd.1, the registered owner of the lands at 1030-1070 East Hastings Street [Lots 4 to 8 of Block 64 District Lot 181 Plan 196; PIDs 015-579-557, 015-579-565, 015-579-573, 015-579-581 and 015-579-590 respectively] to rezone the lands from M-1 (Industrial) District to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District to increase the floor space ratio (FSR) from 5.0 to 16.0 and the building height from 30.5 m (100 ft.) to 112 m (368 ft.), to permit the development of a 37-storey rental residential building containing 382 residential units with commercial space on the ground floor; (ii) Living East Village Investments Inc., the registered owner of the lands located at 1115-1129 East Hastings Street and 360 Glen Drive [Lots 34, 33, 32 and 31 Except the North 5 Feet Now Lane, all of Block 9 of Block A District Lot 182 Plan 355; PIDs 015-366-201, 015-366-189, 004-313-941, and 004-313-925, respectively], to rezone the lands from M-1 (Industrial) District to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District to increase the floor space ratio (FSR) from 5.0 to 23.2 and the building height from 30.5 m (100 ft.) to 116 m (381 ft.), to permit the development of a 39-storey rental residential building containing 385 residential units with commercial space on the ground floor; and (iii) Provincial Rental Housing Corporation, the registered owner of the lands located at 1168-1182 East Hastings Street [Lots 14 to 16, all of Block 14 of Block A District Lot 182 Plan 355; PIDs 007-763-301, 005-599-148 and 015-365-450 respectively], to rezone the lands from M-1 (Industrial) District and RT-3 (Residential) District to CD-1 (Comprehensive Development) District to increase the floor space ratio (FSR) from 5.0 and 3.95 respectively to 10.3 and the building height from 30.5 m (100 ft.) and 8.5 m (28 ft.) respectively to 56 m (184 ft.), to permit the development of a 19-storey social housing building containing 157 units with commercial space on the ground floor generally as presented in the Referral Report dated January 20, 2026, entitled "CD-1 Rezoning: (i) 1030-1070 East Hastings Street, (ii) 1115-1129 East Hastings Street & 360 Glen Drive and (iii) 1168-1182 East Hastings Street", be approved in principle as one application together for all three non-contiguous sites; FURTHER THAT the draft CD-1 By-law, prepared for the Public Hearing in accordance with Appendix A of the above-noted report, be approved in principle; FURTHER THAT the proposed forms of development also be approved in principle, generally as prepared by ZGF Architects, received June 13, 2024; FURTHER THAT the above approvals be subject to the Conditions of Approval contained in Appendices B1, B2 and B3 of the above-noted report; AND FURTHER THAT the Director of Legal Services be instructed to bring forward the draft CD-1 By-laws together for enactment on the same day under one omnibus By-law that will enact all CD-1 By-laws at the same time.

...

FINAL MOTION AS APPROVED

THAT Council refer the application entitled "CD-1 Rezoning: (i) 1030-1070 East Hastings Street, (ii) 1115-1129 East Hastings Street & 360 Glen Drive and (iii) 1168-1182 East Hastings Street", back to staff to study options and considerations related to parking availability, childcare provision, deeper affordability, and heights, densities and building forms, and other matters related to local amenities. The applicant should be included in the discussion, necessitating future consideration by Council.
